title: Supports for suspended solar enhanced oil recovery concentrators and receivers, and associated systems and methods
abstract: Supports for suspended solar enhanced oil recovery concentrators and receivers, and associated systems and methods. A representative solar concentrator system includes a curved reflective element oriented concave relative to a focal line, a curved first rib member carrying the reflective element and oriented concave relative to the focal line, a curved second rib member oriented convex relative to the focal line, and a plurality of cross members coupled between the first rib member and the second rib member. In further embodiments, the system includes a bearing having an inner bearing element in rotational contact with a concentrator attachment member, which supports a solar concentrator. A receiver interface member is fixedly engaged with the inner bearing element, and a receiver attachment member is pivotably connected to the receiver interface member. A biasing element biases the inner bearing element against the receiver.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS l/we claim: 1 . A solar concentrator system, comprising: a curved reflective element oriented concave relative to a focal line; a curved first rib member carrying the reflective element and oriented concave relative to the focal line; a curved second rib member oriented convex relative to the focal line; and a plurality of cross members coupled between the first rib member and the second rib member. 2. The system of claim 1 wherein the curved reflective surface is continuously curved. 3. The system of claim 1 wherein the curved reflective element has a first, reflective surface facing toward the focal line and a second surface facing away from the focal line, and wherein the curved first rib member is attached directly to the second surface of the reflective element. 4. The system of claim 1 wherein the second rib element includes multiple sections and wherein a curvature of a first section is discontinuous with a curvature of a second section. 5. The system of claim 4 wherein the first and second sections are neighboring sections. 6. The system of claim 1 wherein the first rib element has a first stiffness and the second rib element has a second stiffness greater than the first stiffness. 7. The system of claim 6 wherein the curved reflective element includes a central region and an outer region, and wherein the curved reflective element is stiffer in the outer region than in the central region. 8. The system of claim 6 wherein the second rib member is thicker than the first rib member. 9. The system of claim 6 wherein the first rib member is formed from a first material and the second rib member is formed from a second material different than the first material. 10. The system of claim 1 wherein the first rib member and the second rib member together form a first rib, and wherein the system further comprises multiple ribs attached to the reflective element and spaced apart along the focal line. 1 1 . A solar concentrator system, comprising: an enclosure having an overhead support and at least one transmissive surface; an elongated receiver suspended from the overhead support; and an elongated solar concentrator suspended from the receiver, the solar concentrator including: a curved reflective element oriented concave relative to a focal line; a curved first rib member carrying the reflective element and oriented concave relative to the focal line; a curved second rib member oriented convex relative to the focal line; and a plurality of cross members coupled between the first rib member and the second rib member. 12. The system of claim 10 wherein the elongated receiver includes a conduit carrying water. 13. The system of claim 10 wherein the elongated receiver has a first end positioned toward one end of the enclosure and a second end positioned toward a second end of the enclosure, and wherein the elongated receiver is fixedly suspended from the overhead support at a position approximately midway between the first and second ends of the elongated receiver. 14. The system of claim 10, further comprising a drive mechanism coupled to the curved reflective element to rotate the curved reflective element relative to the receiver. 15. A solar concentrator system, comprising: an elongated, tubular receiver having an outer surface with a circumferentially- extending groove; an elongated, trough-shaped solar concentrator; and a bearing coupled to the receiver and the concentrator to support rotation of the concentrator relative to the receiver, the bearing comprising: an inner bearing element positioned in the groove of the receiver and having a first, outwardly-facing bearing surface; a concentrator attachment member having a second, inwardly-facing bearing surface in rotational contact with the outwardly-facing bearing surface of the inner bearing element, the concentrator attachment member having at least one concentrator attachment feature positioned to support the solar concentrator; a receiver interface member fixedly engaged with the inner bearing element; a receiver attachment member pivotably connected to the receiver interface member and having at least one receiver attachment feature positioned to support the receiver in a suspended position; and a biasing element positioned between the receiver and the receiver interface member to bias the inner bearing element against the receiver. 16. The system of claim 15 wherein the receiver attachment member includes first and second projections, and wherein the biasing element has an open C- shape with a first end, a second end, and a central portion between the first and second ends, and wherein the first end is biased into contact with the first projection, the second end is biased into contact with the second projection, and the central portion is biased into contact with the receiver. 17. The system of claim 15 wherein the projections are pins that extend from the first portion of the receiver attachment member. 18. A bearing for a solar concentrator system, comprising: an inner bearing element having a first, outwardly-facing bearing surface; a concentrator attachment member having a second, inwardly-facing bearing surface shaped to be in rotational contact with the outwardly-facing bearing surface of the inner bearing element, the concentrator attachment member having at least one concentrator attachment feature positioned to support a solar concentrator; a receiver interface member positionable to be fixedly engaged with the inner bearing element; a receiver attachment member pivotably connectable to the receiver interface member and having at least one receiver attachment feature positionable to support a solar receiver in a suspended position; and a biasing element positionable between the receiver and the receiver interface member to bias the inner bearing element against the receiver. 19. The bearing of claim 18 wherein the inner bearing element includes first and second separate, circumferentially-extending sections. 20. The bearing of claim 18 wherein the inner bearing element includes a single, circumferentially-extending section having a first end and a second end spaced apart from the first end by a slit. 21 . The bearing of claim 18 wherein the at least one concentrator attachment feature includes a hole. 22. The bearing of claim 18 wherein the at least one receiver attachment feature includes a hole. 23. The bearing of claim 18 wherein the receiver attachment member includes first and second projections, and wherein the biasing element has an open C- shape with a first end positionable to engage the first projection, a second end positioned to engage the second projection, and a central portion between the first and second ends. 24. The bearing of claim 18 wherein: the concentrator element is positioned around the inner bearing element; the receiver interface member is fixedly engaged with the inner bearing element; the receiver attachment member is pivotably connected to the receiver interface member; and the biasing element is engaged with the receiver interface member. 25. A method for making a solar concentrator system, comprising: placing an inner bearing element in a groove of a receiver, the inner bearing element having a first, outwardly-facing bearing surface; placing a second, inwardly-facing bearing surface in rotational contact with the outwardly-facing bearing surface of the inner bearing element, the inwardly-facing bearing surface being carried by a concentrator attachment member; fixedly attaching a receiver interface member to the outwardly-facing bearing surface; and biasing the receiver interface member into engagement with the inner bearing element by positioning a biasing element between the receiver and the receiver interface member. 26. The method of claim 25 wherein positioning the biasing element includes: positioning the biasing element between the receiver interface member and the receiver at a non-normal angle relative to a lengthwise axis of the receiver; and forcing the biasing element to be at a normal angle relative to the receiver. 27. The method of claim 25, further comprising pivotably coupling a receiver attachment member to the receiver interface member. 28. The method of claim 27, further comprising suspending the receiver from an overhead structure by connecting a suspension member between the receiver attachment member and the overhead structure. 29. The method of claim 25, further comprising suspending a solar concentrator from the receiver by connecting a suspension member between the concentrator attachment member and the solar concentrator. 30. A solar concentrator system, comprising: an elongated receiver; an elongated, trough-shaped solar concentrator; and a bearing coupled between the receiver and the concentrator to support rotation of the concentrator relative to the receiver, the bearing comprising: a receiver interface member fixedly attached to the receiver and carrying a first bearing surface; a receiver attachment member pivotably coupled to the receiver interface member; and a concentrator attachment member having a second bearing surface engaged with the first bearing surface, the concentrator attachment member being pivotable relative to the receiver interface member. 31 . The system of claim 30 wherein the receiver interface member includes a first element fixedly attached to the receiver and second element pivotably attached to the first element, and wherein the second element carries the first bearing surface. 32. The system of claim 30 wherein the elongated conduit is elongated along a first axis and wherein the receiver attachment member is pivotable relative to the receiver interface member about a second axis transverse to the first axis. 33. The system of claim 30 wherein the elongated conduit is elongated along a first axis and wherein the concentrator attachment member is pivotable relative to the receiver interface member about a third axis transverse to the first axis. 34. The system of claim 30 wherein the receiver attachment member and the concentrator attachment member are pivotable about different axes relative to the receiver interface member. 35. The system of claim 34 wherein the different axes are generally parallel. 36. The system of claim 30 wherein the elongated conduit is elongated along a first axis and wherein the first and second bearing surfaces are curved about a fourth axis parallel to the first axis. 37. The system of claim 30 wherein the first bearing surface is less than 360 degrees. 38. The system of claim 30 wherein the concentrator attachment member and the receiver attachment member are independently pivotable relative to the receiver. 39. The system of claim 30, further comprising: a first suspension member connected to the receiver attachment member; and a second suspension member connected between the concentrator attachment member and the concentrator. 40. The system of claim 30, further comprising: a plurality of ribs spaced apart along a back surface of the concentrator, wherein individual ribs include: a concave first member; a convex second member, with the first member positioned between the concentrator and the second member; and a plurality of cross members connected between the first and second members. 41 . The system of claim 30 wherein: the concentrator is elongated along a longitudinal axis; the first member has a first thickness when intersected by a plane normal to the longitudinal axis; and the second member has a second thickness, greater than the first thickness, when intersected by the plane. 42. The system of claim 30, further comprising: a compression member elongated along a compression member axis and carried by the concentrator, the compression member having a plurality of attachment locations; a rotary drum carrying a drive line; and a plurality of connecting lines coupled between the drive line and the corresponding plurality of attachment locations, wherein corresponding angles between the connecting lines and the attachment member are sized to cancel reaction forces not aligned with the compression axis when a load is applied to the drive line. 43. A solar concentrator system, comprising: an elongated receiver positioned along a focal line; an elongated concentrator carried by and rotatable relative to the elongated receiver, the elongated concentrator being positioned to reflect incoming light to the focal line, the elongated concentrator including: a plurality of ribs transverse to the focal line; and 

 at least one compression member elongated along a compression axis generally aligned with the focal line, the compression member having a plurality of attachment locations; a drive mechanism; a drive line connected to the drive mechanism; and a plurality of connecting lines coupled between the drive line and the corresponding plurality of attachment locations, wherein corresponding angles between individual connecting lines and the compression member are sized to cancel reaction forces not aligned with the compression axis when a load is applied to the drive line. 44. The system of claim 43 wherein the drive mechanism includes a winch. 45. The system of claim 43 wherein the drive mechanism includes a rotary drum. 46. The system of claim 43 wherein the at least one compression member is a first compression member, the compression axis is a first compression axis, the plurality of attachment locations is a first plurality of attachment locations, the plurality of connecting lines is a first plurality of connecting lines, and wherein the elongated concentrator includes a second compression member elongated along a second compression axis generally aligned with the focal line and having a second plurality of attachment locations, and the system further comprises: a second plurality of connecting lines coupled between the drive line and the corresponding second plurality of attachment locations, wherein corresponding angles between individual connecting lines of the second plurality of connecting lines and the second compression member are sized to cancel reaction forces not aligned with the second compression axis when a load is applied to the drive line. 47. The system of claim 43 wherein the compression member is positioned at an edge of the elongated concentrator. 48. The system of claim 43, further comprising: an enclosure having an overhead support and at least one transmissive surface, and wherein the elongated receiver is suspended from the overhead support. A solar concentrator system, comprising: enclosure having an overhead support and at least one transmissive surface; elongated receiver suspended from the overhead support, the elongated receiver having a first end and a second end, and wherein the elongated receiver is fixedly suspended from the overhead support at an attachment position approximately midway between the first and second ends of the receiver; and elongated solar concentrator suspended from the receiver, and positioned to receive radiation entering the enclosure through the transmissive surface and reflect at least a portion of the radiation to the receiver. 50. The system of claim 49, further comprising a flexible fluid coupling attached to the elongated receiver at the first end. 51 . The system of claim 49 wherein the first and second ends move longitudinally from the attachment position by approximately equal amounts in response to a uniform thermal load on the receiver. 52. The system of claim 49 wherein the elongated solar concentrator is rotatable relative to the elongated receiver, and wherein the system further comprises a bearing positioned between the elongated receiver and the elongated solar concentrator, the bearing comprising: an inner bearing element positioned in a groove of the receiver and having a first, outwardly-facing bearing surface; a concentrator attachment member having a second, inwardly-facing bearing surface in rotational contact with the outwardly-facing bearing surface of the inner bearing element, the concentrator attachment member having 

 at least one concentrator attachment feature positioned to support the solar concentrator; a receiver interface member fixedly engaged with the inner bearing element; a receiver attachment member pivotably connected to the receiver interface member and having at least one receiver attachment feature positioned to support the receiver in a suspended position; and a biasing element positioned between the receiver and the receiver interface member to bias the inner bearing element against the receiver. 53. The system of claim 49, further comprising a drive mechanism coupledoncentrator to rotate the concentrator relative to the receiver. 54. The system of claim 53 wherein the concentrator further includes: a plurality of ribs transverse to the focal line; and at least one compression member elongated along a compression axis generally aligned with the focal line, the compression member having a plurality of attachment locations, and wherein the system further comprises: a drive line connected to the drive mechanism; and a plurality of connecting lines coupled between the drive line and the corresponding plurality of attachment locations, wherein corresponding angles between individual connecting lines and the compression member are sized to cancel reaction forces not aligned with the compression axis when a load is applied to the drive line. 55. The system of claim 49 wherein the receiver includes: a curved reflective element oriented concave relative to a focal line; a curved first rib member carrying the reflective element and oriented concave relative to the focal line; a curved second rib member oriented convex relative to the focal line; and a plurality of cross members coupled between the first rib member and the second rib member.
